text.title: ADR Staleness Audit Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ADR Staleness Audit` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every audited ADR is scored on 4 signals: (1) code-reality match, (2) stakeholder still present, (3) reversal triggers fired, (4) external context change. Scoring < 3/4 → status change.
rule.rationale: Multi-signal rubrics catch drift one-signal heuristics miss.

rule.statement: Status transitions MUST use the closed set: accepted | superseded(→link) | deprecated | re-open. Custom statuses forbidden.
rule.rationale: Custom statuses fragment tooling and tribal knowledge.

rule.statement: 'superseded' transitions MUST link the new ADR. No-link superseded = INCOMPLETE.
rule.rationale: Unlinked supersession breaks the decision graph.

rule.statement: Audit runs quarterly; teams with > 50 ADRs may run monthly. Yearly is too slow.
rule.rationale: ADRs decay measurably across 90-180 day windows in active codebases.

rule.statement: Audit output is committed as `adr-audit-YYYY-Q<N>.md` in the repo with the named auditor.
When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
